testing_tools: OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite, Custom Scripts
test_scenarios: ["Attempt to bypass MFA by directly calling protected API endpoints with a valid password-only session token","Privilege escalation attempt: Vendor role attempting to access System Admin endpoints (e.g., /api/v1/admin/*)","JWT manipulation: Attempt to modify payload claims (role/userId) and re-sign with 'none' algorithm","Token Replay: Attempt to use a revoked Refresh Token to generate new Access Tokens after logout"]
acceptance_criteria: All unauthorized access attempts must return HTTP 401/403. No privilege escalation possible.
reporting_requirements: Pass/Fail report per role matrix combination

test_scenarios: ["Race condition simulation: Concurrent payout approval requests for the same milestone","Idempotency check: Replay Stripe 'payment_intent.succeeded' webhook multiple times","Saga Compensation: Simulate Wise API failure after internal ledger deduction to verify rollback/compensation logic","Parameter tampering: Attempt to modify payout currency or amount in the API payload to differ from the approved milestone amount"]
acceptance_criteria: Transactions must be exactly once. Ledger must balance. No double payouts.

compliance_requirement: GDPR Art 17 (Right to Erasure), SOC2 CC6.7
testing_tools: AWS Macie, Presidio, Custom Python Scripts
test_scenarios: ["Upload SOW documents containing known PII (SSN, Email, Phone) and verify redacted output from AI worker","Prompt Injection: Attempt to inject instructions into SOW text to bypass sanitization or exfiltrate system instructions","Verify vector embeddings database (pgvector) does not contain raw PII in metadata fields","GDPR Erasure: Verify 'Right to be Forgotten' request completely removes user data from relational DB, logs, and vector indices"]
acceptance_criteria: Zero PII persistence in non-secure storage. AI must not output raw PII.
trend_analysis: False negative rate of PII detection

test_scenarios: ["Upload malicious files (e.g., Malware, Webshells) disguised as PDF/DOCX","Path traversal attack via filename manipulation (e.g., '../../etc/passwd')","Upload 'Zip bomb' to test worker resource exhaustion resilience","Verify S3 bucket policies prevent public access to uploaded SOWs"]
acceptance_criteria: Malicious files blocked/quarantined. No execution on server. Private S3 access only.

test_scenarios: ["Verify RDS is encrypted at rest and not publicly accessible","Check EKS Control Plane logging is enabled","Verify Security Groups restrict access to least privilege (e.g., DB only accepts traffic from App/Worker SG)","Scan for hardcoded secrets in codebase and Terraform state"]
acceptance_criteria: 100% compliance with defined CIS benchmarks for EKS and RDS.
